Belgium’s Sylvère Maes, the overall winner the year before, won an ITT that started in Lons-le-Saunier. In the afternoon, the race went from Champagnole to Genève, and the reigning Swiss champion Léo Amberg took the stage. A few weeks later, he was the first Swiss to stand on the overall podium of the Tour, 3rd of an edition dominated by France’s Roger Lapébie.
Champagnole also hosted a start in 1964 (Jan Janssen won in Thonon-les-Bains) and another finish in 2020, after 166.5 km from Bourg-en-Bresse. Soren Kragh Andersen attacked in the finale to take his second stage win in this Tour, the 21st for Denmark. The nation is up to 30 stage wins after Mads Pedersen’s victory in Foix last week.
Visited by the Tour de l’Avenir on several occasions, Champagnole was notably on the map in 2021, giving way to stage 6 en route to Septmoncel, where twins Anders and Tobias Johannessen achieved a spectacular 1-2.
Most recently, Champagnole was the start town of stage 7 of the Tour de France Femmes avec Zwift 2024. The polka-dot jersey on her back, Justine Ghekiere attacked early in the stage and powered to a solo victory at Le Grand-Bornand. Kasia Niewiadoma-Phinney retained the Maillot Jaune on the eve of her exhilarating triumph at L’Alpe Belgium’s Sylvère Maes, the overall winner the year before, won an ITT that started in Lons-le-Saunier. In the afternoon, the race went from Champagnole to Genève, and the reigning Swiss champion Léo Amberg took the stage. A few weeks later, he was the first Swiss to stand on the overall podium of the Tour, 3rd of an edition dominated by France’s Roger Lapébie.
Champagnole also hosted a start in 1964 (Jan Janssen won in Thonon-les-Bains) and another finish in 2020, after 166.5 km from Bourg-en-Bresse. Soren Kragh Andersen attacked in the finale to take his second stage win in this Tour, the 21st for Denmark. The nation is up to 30 stage wins after Mads Pedersen’s victory in Foix last week.
Visited by the Tour de l’Avenir on several occasions, Champagnole was notably on the map in 2021, giving way to stage 6 en route to Septmoncel, where twins Anders and Tobias Johannessen achieved a spectacular 1-2.
Most recently, Champagnole was the start town of stage 7 of the Tour de France Femmes avec Zwift 2024. The polka-dot jersey on her back, Justine Ghekiere attacked early in the stage and powered to a solo victory at Le Grand-Bornand. Kasia Niewiadoma-Phinney retained the Maillot Jaune on the eve of her exhilarating triumph at L’Alpe Belgium’s Sylvère Maes, the overall winner the year before, won an ITT that started in Lons-le-Saunier. In the afternoon, the race went from Champagnole to Genève, and the reigning Swiss champion Léo Amberg took the stage. A few weeks later, he was the first Swiss to stand on the overall podium of the Tour, 3rd of an edition dominated by France’s Roger Lapébie.
Champagnole also hosted a start in 1964 (Jan Janssen won in Thonon-les-Bains) and another finish in 2020, after 166.5 km from Bourg-en-Bresse. Soren Kragh Andersen attacked in the finale to take his second stage win in this Tour, the 21st for Denmark. The nation is up to 30 stage wins after Mads Pedersen’s victory in Foix last week.
Visited by the Tour de l’Avenir on several occasions, Champagnole was notably on the map in 2021, giving way to stage 6 en route to Septmoncel, where twins Anders and Tobias Johannessen achieved a spectacular 1-2.
Most recently, Champagnole was the start town of stage 7 of the Tour de France Femmes avec Zwift 2024. The polka-dot jersey on her back, Justine Ghekiere attacked early in the stage and powered to a solo victory at Le Grand-Bornand. Kasia Niewiadoma-Phinney retained the Maillot Jaune on the eve of her exhilarating triumph at L’Alpe
